Series_Season: Inside the Mind of a Pro — 2024 WSOP
Preflop_Action: A masked early-position player opens; Mateos makes a deliberately loose deep-stack call from the hijack with K5s; big blind also calls.
Flop_Action: Big blind and raiser check; Mateos bets 1,500 into 4,800. Big blind folds and the raiser calls.
Turn: Trips-favoring card for the preflop raiser; exact card unknown
Turn_Action: Villain checks. Mateos considers barreling but checks back because the card improves the raiser's range heavily.
River: Four cards to a ten-high straight
River_Action: Villain checks. Holding the bottom of his range, Mateos bets 7,700 into 7,800 to represent a straight; villain quickly folds.
Outcome: Mateos wins with a near-pot river bluff.
Spot_Type: Deep speculative call / polarized river bluff
Live_Read_or_Exploit: Mateos identified many automatic folds after the small flop call and used a river where his value range could credibly be almost entirely straights.
Key_Lesson: When you reach the river with the absolute bottom of your range, ask whether the runout gives you a credible polarized story and enough natural folds to justify a large bluff.
